title: Scope of remediation for issue #94 (rules corpus audit)
context: Validation against the current codebase + online research shows most of issue #94 is already implemented. Finding #1 (correctness bugs in boost.md and rust-web/security) was fixed in PR #93 and the fix is factually correct per Laravel Boost docs and serde docs. Finding #3 (workflow specs) and Finding #6 (regression guard) are covered by an existing test guard tests/unit/content/rule-quality.test.ts which blocks ## Trigger / ### Step / run_id / .paqad/ in rules (the 3 workflow specs are deliberately allowlisted). Remaining, NOT yet enforced: Finding #2 (per-stack architecture.md/modules.md still duplicate module-map.yml boundary ownership, though they now defer to it) and Finding #4 (~45-75 short 'platitude' files). Market best practices (Cursor/AGENTS.md 2026) validate the lean-actionable-non-duplicative direction.

options.label: Extend existing guard only
options.detail: Extend rule-quality.test.ts to enforce the two remaining acceptance criteria (no new module-boundary duplication; platitude-shape rejection) as a durable backstop. Complements existing feature, low risk, no mass deletion.

options.label: Guard + surgical prune
options.detail: Extend the guard AND delete/collapse the confirmed platitude + duplicative files in the same PR.

options.label: Full issue as written
options.detail: Move workflow specs out of rules/, mass-delete ~45 platitudes, backfill stub stacks, collapse all duplication. Largest, riskiest; reverses some deliberate team choices (allowlist).

options.label: Close as largely-done
options.detail: Comment on #94 that criteria 1/2/3/6 are met; close or narrow it.

rationale: User selected Guard + surgical prune: extend the existing rule-quality.test.ts guard to enforce the two unmet acceptance criteria (no new per-stack module-boundary duplication; platitude-shape rejection) AND delete/collapse the confirmed platitude + duplicative rule files in the same PR.
